dossier.overview:
Island Dreamforge is a local fantasy-map-to-terrain studio in the monorepo. You upload a handmade color map, assign heights in meters, tune procedural materials and water, optionally stack overlay layers, sculpt in a Three.js viewport, and export game-ready height maps, meshes, and packaged ZIP archives for web portals or the C++ game.

Run it with ./mapmkr run from island-dreamforge/ (frontend on port 5173, FastAPI backend on 8000). Projects autosave to browser IndexedDB and survive refresh until you hit Reset project.

sections.title: App guide
sections.summary: What Island Dreamforge does, how the five-step wizard works, and what it exports.

blocks.body:
Objective: turn clean 2D color maps into believable 3D island terrain with shoreline water, procedural ground materials, optional structure/marker overlays, and deterministic exports sized for real-world meters.

Island Dreamforge sits upstream of Pokémon Resort overworld content. Named presets in shared/island_presets.json (pokemon_resort_soft_cliffs, volcanic_island, gentle_resort_island) tune height generation for resort-style islands.

Stack:
• Backend — FastAPI + NumPy + Pillow + trimesh (height pipeline, bathymetry, mesh export)
• Frontend — React 18 + Vite + Three.js (five-step wizard, 3D editor, autosave)
• Runner — ./mapmkr (setup, doctor, run, build, clean)

blocks.title: Data flow
blocks.caption: Color map in → packaged assets out
blocks.source:
flowchart LR
    Map[Handmade color PNG] --> H[Step 1 Heights]
    H --> T[Step 2 Textures]
    T --> W[Step 3 Water]
    W --> L[Step 4 Layers]
    L --> E[Step 5 3D Export]
    E --> Web[Web portal ZIP]
    E --> Game[Game package ZIP]
    E --> Mesh[GLB OBJ STL PLY]

blocks.body:
The UI is a linear wizard orchestrated in frontend/src/App.jsx. Each stage persists into the same IndexedDB autosave key (island-dreamforge-autosave-v7).

1 · Heights — Upload a base map. Click a map color and type its height in meters. The app starts with water at 0 m, samples pixels exactly, and generates smooth height bands from your color samples. Island world scale sets land width/depth (presets up to ~30 km), mesh density, and fixed-meter spacing for trees and future paths.

2 · Textures — Configure the procedural art pass before final water editing: resolution, tile size, macro variation, normal/bump strength, tree density, rock steepness, gravel, sand bands, wet sand, and transition softness. Preview swatches show water, trees, rock, gravel, sand, and grass.

3 · Water — Tune ocean disc radius, shelf depths, foam, bathymetry smoothing, and water color steps. Water baking is non-destructive: default mode is Visual water only; indent modes carve a shallow bed instead of punching holes through the terrain.

4 · Layers — Optional overlays: water hints, structures, markers/POIs, texture/noise layers. Each layer can be analyzed (connected components), hidden, cleared, deleted, or exported as JSON.

5 · 3D / Export — Orbit terrain in Move mode by default; switch to sculpt/paint when needed. Regenerate procedural textures, refresh derived maps (island mask, water depth, material IDs), and download meshes or full packages.

blocks.caption: Stage navigation — five wizard tabs
blocks.body:
<nav className="stage-tabs">
        <button className={stage === 1 ? 'active' : ''} onClick={() => setStage(1)}>1 · Heights</button>
        <button className={stage === 2 ? 'active' : ''} onClick={() => setStage(2)}>2 · Textures</button>
        <button className={stage === 3 ? 'active' : ''} onClick={() => setStage(3)}>3 · Water</button>
        <button className={stage === 4 ? 'active' : ''} onClick={() => setStage(4)}>4 · Layers</button>
        <button className={stage === 5 ? 'active' : ''} onClick={() => setStage(5)}>5 · 3D / Export</button>
      </nav>

Exports fall into three groups: quick downloads from step 5, standalone mesh formats via /api/export-mesh, and packaged ZIP archives with manifest.json for downstream tools.

Web Portal Package — GLB scene, RGBA material splat, water depth/color/foam textures, island/shoreline/ocean masks, shore-distance map, world.json, materials.json, structure/marker JSON stubs.

Game Package — Quantized PNG maps (height u16, collision, material IDs, water depth, bathymetry, seafloor), chunked terrain LOD binaries (IDFMAP01), seafloor LOD meshes, coastline skirt (IDFSKRT1), preview GLB, and game/world.json.

Ad-hoc — Height PNG stages, painted texture PNG, normal map PNG, water mask PNG, layer JSON, GLB/OBJ/STL/PLY meshes, full project ZIP via /api/export-project.

blocks.html:
ExportFormatTypical use
Height map stages16-bit PNGAuthoring checkpoints, external DCC
Procedural texturePNGAlbedo painted in viewport
Normal mapPNGIncluded in full project export
Water maskPNGWhen a water layer is active
Structure / marker dataJSONPlaced objects from layer analysis
Terrain meshGLB, OBJ, STL, PLYSingle-mesh download
Web portal packageZIP + manifest.jsonThree.js / web viewers
Game packageZIP + chunked .binPokémon Resort C++ loader path
Full projectZIPArchive all stages and settings

blocks.caption: Export manifest header — shared by web and game ZIP packages
blocks.body:
manifest = {
        "format": "island-dreamforge-export",
        "schemaVersion": 1,
        "version": 1,
        "profile": profile,
        "package": package_name,
        "createdBy": "Island Dreamforge",
        "units": "meters",
        "coordinateSystem": {
            "horizontalAxes": ["x", "z"],
            "verticalAxis": "y",
            "origin": "world_center",
        },
        "world": meta["world"],
        "ocean": meta["ocean"],
        "detail": _detail_metadata(opts, profile),
        "materials": {
            "ids": _material_names(),
            "splatChannels": {
                "r": "sand_wet_sand",
                "g": "grass_forest",
                "b": "rock_gravel",
                "a": "water_seafloor",
            },
        },

From island-dreamforge/:

./mapmkr run

The runner checks Python 3.10–3.13 and Node 18+, creates the backend venv if needed, installs dependencies, starts Uvicorn on http://127.0.0.1:8000 and Vite on http://127.0.0.1:5173, and prints log paths under .mapmkr/logs/.

Other commands: ./mapmkr setup, ./mapmkr doctor, ./mapmkr build, ./mapmkr clean. Docker compose is available but local dev normally uses mapmkr.

Override the API URL in the frontend with VITE_API_URL if the backend port changes.

blocks.caption: Quick start
blocks.body:
## Run locally

```bash
./mapmkr run
```

The runner checks for Python 3.10-3.13 and Node 18+, creates the backend virtual environment, installs missing dependencies, starts the FastAPI backend and Vite frontend, and prints the local URL.

sections.title: Architecture
sections.summary: Backend modules, frontend ownership, and HTTP surface.

blocks.title: Runtime layout
blocks.caption: Default ports in local dev
blocks.source:
flowchart TB
    subgraph FE["Frontend :5173"]
      App[App.jsx wizard + autosave]
      TV[TerrainViewport.jsx 3D scene]
    end
    subgraph BE["Backend :8000"]
      T[terrain.py heights + water bake]
      IM[island_maps.py masks + bathymetry]
      ME[mesh_export.py GLB + chunked bins]
      IE[island_export.py ZIP packages]
    end
    App -->|multipart POST| BE
    TV --> App

blocks.html:
ModuleResponsibility
backend/app/terrain.pyColor quantization, preprocess, height generation, smoothing, water bake
backend/app/island_maps.pyIsland mask, shoreline, shore distance, bathymetry, material maps
backend/app/layers.pyConnected components on overlay layers
backend/app/mesh_export.pyHeight grid → trimesh; GLB/OBJ/STL/PLY; IDFMAP01 chunked game mesh
backend/app/island_export.pyWeb/game ZIP assembly and manifest.json
frontend/src/App.jsxFive-step UI, IndexedDB autosave, API calls
frontend/src/TerrainViewport.jsxThree.js scene, materials, water shader, trees, brush tools
shared/island_presets.jsonNamed height-generation presets
shared/viewport_config.jsonCinematic lighting, fog, ocean, forest density

blocks.body: HTTP routes are documented in island-dreamforge/docs/API.md. Key groups: /api/analyze-colors and /api/heightmap (step 1), /api/bake-water (step 3), /api/analyze-layer (step 4), /api/export-mesh and /api/export-project (step 5), /api/island-derived-maps, /api/export-web-island, /api/export-game-island.

sections.title: Materials & 3D viewport
sections.summary: Procedural ground types, water shader, and world scale.

blocks.body:
The 3D viewport hides sea-level terrain pixels so the island reads as land rising from water rather than a flat rectangular PNG. Water uses the Three.js Water addon with transparency, reflection, depth darkening, shoreline foam, and horizon fade.

Paint materials in the viewport (trees, grass, sand, gravel, rock) map to export material IDs 0–7: water/seafloor, wet sand, sand, grass, forest, rock, gravel, dirt/path. Material splat RGBA channels group sand, grass/forest, rock/gravel, and water/seafloor for web exports.

Structures and markers snap to the final smoothed terrain surface. Vegetation uses instanced forest clumps driven by slope and height rules from shared/viewport_config.json.

blocks.caption: Standalone mesh download formats

sections.title: UI walkthrough
sections.summary: Captured from the running local app (./mapmkr run on port 5173).

images.caption: 1 · Heights — color samples and world scale
4 · Layers — structure, marker, and overlay stacks

blocks.html:
Path in ZIPDescription
manifest.jsonFormat island-dreamforge-export v1, world/ocean/detail, file index
game/world.jsonWorld width, depth, sea level, export profile metadata
game/height_u16.png16-bit height field in meters
game/collision_height_u16.pngLand-only collision heights (ocean flattened to sea level)
game/material_ids_u8.pngPer-texel material index 0–7
game/water_depth_u8.pngNormalized water depth
game/bathymetry_u8.pngSeafloor shape preview
game/seafloor_height_u16.pngSeafloor elevation below sea level
game/terrain_lod0.bin … lod2Chunked land meshes (IDFMAP01)
game/seafloor_lod0.bin, lod1Chunked seafloor meshes
game/coastline_skirt.binCoastline skirt mesh (IDFSKRT1)
preview/island_game_preview.glbCombined preview scene
game/structure_instances.jsonStructure placements (when authored)
game/marker_instances.jsonPOI / marker placements

blocks.body: Export detail presets (Preview Low/Medium/High, Web Export High, Game Export Low/Medium/High) control mesh resolution and chunk size. Game chunk size defaults to 16 quads and is configurable in the Export Profiles panel on step 5.

Authoritative markdown inside island-dreamforge/:

• README.md — product behavior, exports, water rules
• docs/ARCHITECTURE.md — module map and extension points
• docs/API.md — HTTP route reference
• docs/ASSETS.md — texture and skybox paths
• AGENTS.md — coding-agent task routing

Related Resort Docs articles: owmap-format (overworld map binary consumed by the game), charbin-schema (character packages).

Sibling apps: pokemon-resort/ (C++ runtime), pokemon-resort-page/ (site + Operations Desk), spmk/ (sprite tooling).
